items.mistake: Fitting StandardScaler (or feature selection) on all data before CV
items.fix: Wrap preprocessing and model in a Pipeline, or refit transforms inside each fold on training indices only.
items.why: Validation-fold statistics leak into training, inflating CV scores—same leakage class as preprocessing before hold-out splits.
items.example: `scaler.fit(X)` then `cross_val_score(model, scaler.transform(X), y)` — validation folds saw global mean and variance.

items.mistake: Using plain k-fold on severely imbalanced classification
items.fix: Use StratifiedKFold (or stratified equivalents) so each fold contains all classes.
items.why: A fold missing the positive class makes recall, PR-AUC, or F1 undefined or meaningless for that round.
items.example: 0.5% fraud rate: one of five folds had zero fraud rows; mean AUC looked fine but one fold was NaN.

items.mistake: Reporting the inner CV best score as final performance
items.fix: Use nested CV: inner loop picks hyperparameters; outer loop scores on held-out folds never used for tuning.
items.why: Selecting the best of many settings on the same folds optimistically biases the reported metric.
items.example: Grid-search 50 random forest configs on the same 5 folds, then report the top mean CV AUC as unbiased test performance.

items.mistake: Shuffling time-series rows for standard k-fold
items.fix: Use temporal or walk-forward splits that train on past and validate on future.
items.why: Random k-fold lets future information appear in training folds, producing optimistic scores for forecasting tasks.
items.example: Daily sales forecast with shuffled 5-fold CV shows RMSE 12; walk-forward validation on the last month shows RMSE 28.
